preamble: The shift from the open central/back vowel [a] to the near-open front vowel [æ] is a common historical process known as fronting or palatalization. It frequently occurs when influenced by surrounding palatal consonants or through broad systemic vowel shifts.

examples.note: Anglo-Frisian Brightening: Proto-Germanic short [a] shifted to [æ].
Imala: The 'leaning' of the open vowel toward a fronted quality in certain dialects.
Vowel Synharmonism: Fronting of the back vowel after a palatal consonant.
